summary: Frontend-only regression suite after Path A integration (mtkclient subprocess wrapper + WebSocket bridge + useCliBridge.runJob() + AppContext live-mode preference). All 10 regression/verification items PASS. Web app did NOT regress — demo mode still works end-to-end, no spurious WebSocket attempts in default mode, and bridge-enabled mode degrades gracefully with reconnect throttled to 5s.

visible_panels_in_screenshot: Connection / Active Operation / Successful Ops / Exploit DB stat tiles (4)
REG1_admin_login_and_dashboard.note: Some panels don't expose data-testid (device-status, device-info, quick-actions, console, get-aether-hero) — flagged as minor enhancement. Confirmed visible in screenshots.

REG3_mobile_drawer_375px.note: [data-testid='sidebar'] not present on Sidebar root — bounding_box test failed but drawer behaviour visually verified.

REG5_demo_mode_operation_bypass_frp.progress_indicator_updates: 13% observed in screenshot, increments over time

REG6_download_popovers.hero_card_download_button: DOWNLOAD .MSI (WINDOWS) button + .DMG/.APPIMAGE links visible in dashboard hero (screenshot)

REG8_no_errors_in_default_mode.wss_to_emergent_ws_present: that is the React/HMR/bundler socket — NOT from useCliBridge
REG8_no_errors_in_default_mode.verdict: PASS — useCliBridge correctly skips connect() when enabled !== '1'

REG9_settings_page.note: Disconnect button appears on Dashboard device-card (visible when connected) rather than Settings — verified in screenshot. Not a regression.

V10_bridge_enabled_offline_behavior.error_text: WebSocket connection to 'ws://127.0.0.1:8765/' failed: net::ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED — these are Chromium-emitted errors from the failed WS, NOT JS thrown errors. Expected behavior.
V10_bridge_enabled_offline_behavior.no_error_flood: yes — reconnect throttled to 5s interval as designed

critical_code_review_comments: useCliBridge.js correctly gates the WebSocket creation behind `localStorage.getItem('aether.bridge.enabled') === '1'` (line 39). No connect attempt made in default mode — verified 0 WS attempts to 127.0.0.1:8765 in 6s observation window. ✓

AppContext.runAction correctly prefers cliBridge.runJob() ONLY when `cliBridge.status === 'connected'` (line 100). When offline, falls back to ACTION_LOG_TEMPLATES demo. Verified console pane logs '>>> EXECUTING (DEMO):' not 'LIVE'. ✓
BRIDGE_METHODS map (lines 12-18) covers bypass_frp, unlock_bootloader, erase_userdata, read_info — repair_imei intentionally omitted pending a future input modal. Sensible. ✓
On WS close, useCliBridge correctly rejects all pendingRef promises and resolves all jobSubsRef with exit_code: -1 so React components don't hang (lines 102-108). ✓
Reconnect setTimeout is 5000ms (line 109) — confirmed no error flood observed across 14s, ~3 attempts max in default test window. ✓
StatusBar cli-bridge-status has three states: disabled/offline/connecting/connected with appropriate text + colour dot. ✓

ENHANCEMENT (non-blocking): Several dashboard top-level panels lack `data-testid` (device-status, device-info, quick-actions, console pane, get-aether-hero card, stat-tiles). Adding these would make automated regression tests less screenshot-dependent.
ENHANCEMENT (non-blocking): The `repair_imei` BRIDGE_METHODS entry is commented out (line 17). When that modal lands, also wire it into BRIDGE_METHODS so live mode works for IMEI repair.

ENHANCEMENT (non-blocking): The Chromium-level 'WebSocket failed: ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED' message cannot be suppressed from JS — consider mentioning this in docs so users in bridge-enabled mode don't think the app is broken. (Status bar already says 'CLI · offline', which is the user-facing signal.)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Path A integration (mtkclient subprocess + WebSocket bridge + useCliBridge.runJob + AppContext.runAction live-mode) verified non-regressive on the web app. Demo mode end-to-end fully functional. Bridge-enabled mode (localStorage.aether.bridge.enabled='1') gracefully degrades to CLI · offline with 5s reconnect throttle and no JS errors. The only console output in offline-reconnect mode is the unavoidable Chromium-level 'WebSocket failed: ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED' which is expected behaviour. No code changes required. If you continue testing, focus areas would be: (1) IMEI repair modal when implemented, (2) actual mtkclient subprocess invocation (requires Python+mtkclient+phone — out of scope for web testing), (3) adding data-testid to top-level dashboard panels for easier future automation.
